title: Bisection Root Visualizer
description: An interactive numerical analysis sandbox demonstrating the Bisection Method for root finding, featuring IVT boundary validation, step-by-step interval reduction, real-time convergence telemetry, and a shrinking bracket visualizer.
author: Girish Madarkar
features: Sandboxed expression evaluator: new Function('x', 'return ' + expr) with NaN/Infinity guard, supports x²-4, x³-x-2, cos(x)-x presets
IVT boundary validation: instantaneous f(a)·f(b) product check on every bound change, locks controls and triggers CSS shake animation on violation
Single-step computation: midpoint c = (a+b)/2, f(c) evaluated, interval narrowed by sign product f(a)·f(c) vs f(b)·f(c), displayed in real time
Run to convergence: requestAnimationFrame-driven asynchronous loop iterating until |b-a| < tolerance, button disable while running
Shrinking bracket visualizer: three color-coded draggable markers (cyan a, emerald c, magenta b) on a horizontal bar with percentage-based positioning that narrows with each step
Historical log table: iteration count, current a/b/c, f(c), interval width logged as table rows with sticky header and auto-scroll
Convergence detection: when |b-a| drops below tolerance slider value (0.0001-0.1), locks step buttons and displays ROOT ≈ c to 6 decimals
Equation preset defaults: each preset loads recommended [a,b] bounds (x²-4: [0,4], x³-x-2: [1,2], cos(x)-x: [0,1.5])
Clear pipeline: resets iteration counter, flushes log table, hides midpoint marker/root display, reverts bounds to preset defaults without page reload
